reasoning:
The forecast evaluates whether Aravindh Chithambaram will have a ranking of 11th or better (as high or higher than his June 8 ranking of 11th) on four future dates. 
1. June 15: FIDE publishes rankings monthly. The June 1 list (where he is 11) will still be active. Probability is set very high (0.95). 
2. July 8: He recently gained 8.8 rating points after winning a tournament on June 7, pushing his live rating to 9th. Because this cushion puts him solidly above 11th and July 1 is nearby, he has a strong chance to retain top 11 in the July list (0.85). 
3. Sept 6: He is 25 years old and just hit a career peak. While his current form is excellent, ratings among the top 10-20 (between 2740-2760) are extremely compressed, and reversion to the mean is common after sharp peaks. As top players are highly active, there's a fair chance he gets over-taken by historically stronger players over 3 months (0.60). 
4. Dec 5: Over a 6-month horizon, a first-time top-10 player without an extended track record at this level is more likely to revert slightly (e.g., dropping to 12th-15th). Hence, the probability drops slightly below even (0.48). Uncertainty increases with the duration of the forecast.
